disclosure_items.clinical_fact: One-week history of a spreading, hot, painful red area on the right lower leg that began at the site of a minor scrape
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me about your leg?
When did the redness start and has it changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: About a week ago I got a small scrape on my lower leg, and since then it has gone red, hot and really sore, and the red area keeps getting bigger.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Subjective fever with a home thermometer reading of 38.2 degrees, feeling hot and sweaty
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel feverish or have you taken your temperature?
Are you feeling hot, sweaty or unwell in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I feel hot and sweaty and a bit shivery. My home thermometer said 38.2.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Palpitations and feeling generally unwell (a proxy for the systemic inflammatory response)
trigger_question_examples: Is your heart racing or pounding?
Do you feel unwell in yourself overall?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My heart has been racing and I feel pretty rotten overall — I don't know how much of it is the leg and how much is how anxious I am.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Deliberate one-week delay in seeking care driven by distrust of the healthcare system and panic in clinical settings
trigger_question_examples: What stopped you getting it looked at sooner?
How do you feel about seeing a doctor or going to hospital?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I put it off for days because clinics make me panic and I've been dismissed and disbelieved before, so I didn't want to go through that again.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Self-treating the leg with an unprescribed herbal poultice instead of seeking care
trigger_question_examples: Have you put anything on it or taken anything for it?
Are you using any home or herbal remedies?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been putting a herbal poultice on it that I trust more than tablets, but it hasn't made any difference.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Exquisite tenderness of the affected area and difficulty weight-bearing on the leg
trigger_question_examples: How painful is it, and can you walk on it?
What happens when you touch or move the leg?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's really painful — even light touch hurts a lot, and it's hard to put weight on that leg.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No confusion, no breathlessness and no collapse at present (pertinent negatives that do not exclude progression)
trigger_question_examples: Have you felt confused, faint or short of breath?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I don't feel confused or short of breath, and I haven't had the violent shivering shakes — just hot, sore and anxious.

patient_initiated_exchanges.trigger_condition: At any point in the consultation, especially early or if she senses hesitation
patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: I know you probably think this is just a rash or that I'm overreacting, but look at my notes — please take this seriously.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explicitly validates that the concern is real and legitimate, does not dismiss or minimise, explains plainly why an in-person look at the leg is needed today, and addresses the mistrust with concrete accommodations (advocate, bringing records, shared monitoring).

deflection_behaviours.description: Becomes guarded and defensive if she feels dismissed or minimised; references past experiences of bias and may threaten to disengage or refuse in-person care.
deflection_behaviours.trigger_condition: The AI Doctor attributes her symptoms to anxiety alone, calls it 'just a rash', or minimises her concern.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: See, this is exactly what always happens — you think I'm exaggerating. Why should I bother going in if no one listens?
